sections.content: Functions are reusable blocks of code designed to perform specific tasks.
A function is a self-contained block of code that performs a specific operation.
Predefined functions provided by C libraries such as printf(), scanf(), strlen(), sqrt().
Functions created by programmers according to application requirements.
A function declaration informs the compiler about the function before its use.
Contains the actual body of the function.
Transfers control to the function for execution.

sections.section_title: Types Based on Arguments and Return Values

sections.content: The return statement sends a value back to the calling function.
Values passed during a function call.
Variables that receive values inside the function definition.
A copy of the variable is passed to the function.
The address of the variable is passed to the function.

summary: Functions can be library functions or user-defined functions.
Functions may accept arguments and return values.
Call by value and call by reference are important concepts.
